source_docs.title: VASA-seq Supplementary Table 14: reagents and equipment
VASA-seq Supplementary Table 12: oligos, probes, and dual-index primers

oligos.name: inDrop v3 barcoded polyacrylamide bead (oligo-dT capture primer)
oligos.role: Photocleavable bead capture / RT primer carrying the split-pool cell barcode, UFI (UMI) and oligo-dT; primes reverse transcription of poly(A)-tailed RNA fragments

components.name: PCR/PE handle + T7 promoter + Read handle
components.role: 5' bead handle; the i7 indexing primer common region (GGGTGTCGGGTGCAG) anneals here and T7 drives IVT — exact bead sequence per inDrop v3 (Zilionis 2017 / Briggs 2018), not printed in these documents

components.name: Cell barcode 1 (BC1)
components.role: first split-pool barcode round; read in the i7 index read

components.name: W1 linker
components.role: inDrop v3 W1 adapter separating BC1 and BC2 (canonical GAGTGATTGCTTGTGACGCCTT; not printed here)

components.name: Cell barcode 2 (BC2)
components.role: second split-pool barcode round; read in Read2

components.name: UFI / UMI
components.role: 6-nt unique fragment identifier for strand-specific molecule counting

components.name: oligo-dT
components.role: poly(dT) tail hybridizing the poly(A) added to RNA fragments

oligos.notes: Cell barcode = BC1(8) + BC2(8) = 16 nt; UFI/UMI = 6 nt (stated in FASTQ pre-processing). Beads made per Zilionis 2017; barcoded by Bst 2.0 extension with the inDrop v3 scheme (Briggs 2018). Barcode is UV-photocleaved off the bead inside the droplet before RT.

oligos.name: RA3 oligonucleotide for VASA-drop
oligos.role: 3' adapter ligated to the amplified RNA (aRNA) 3' end by T4 RNA Ligase 2 truncated; provides the RTP priming site
oligos.notes: Listed as 5rApp/TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGG/3SpC3 — 5'-adenylated (App) and 3' C3-spacer blocked for pre-adenylated, ATP-independent single-strand ligation. Table S12; 'this study'.

oligos.name: RTP oligonucleotide for VASA-drop
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer annealing to the ligated RA3 adapter to make cDNA of the aRNA (final-library RT step)
oligos.notes: Reverse-complementary to the RA3 adapter (RA3 = TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGG). Table S12; 'this study'. Used with SuperScript III at 50 °C.

oligos.name: VASA-drop dual-index PCR primer, i5 / P5 (VD_ILMN8_i5_1–46)
oligos.role: P5 indexing PCR primer (i5 read = Index2); adds P5 + 8-nt i5 sample index + Read1/s5 handle

components.name: i5 index
components.role: 8-nt sample index (e.g. VD_ILMN8_i5_1 = AGCGCTAG, i5_2 = GATATCGA, i5_3 = CGCAGACG …; 46 provided)

components.name: s5 / Read1 handle
components.role: annealing/Read1 sequencing-primer region on the RA3/RTP (cDNA) side of the library

oligos.notes: 46 i5 primers listed in Supplementary Table 12; unique dual indexing used to detect index hopping on NovaSeq.

oligos.name: VASA-drop dual-index PCR primer, i7 / P7 (VD_ILMN8_i7_1–46)
oligos.role: P7 indexing PCR primer (i7 read = Index1); adds P7 + 8-nt i7 sample index + 15-bp inDrop common handle

components.name: i7 index
components.role: 8-nt second sample index inserted directly after the 15-bp common sequence (e.g. VD_ILMN8_i7_1 = AACCGCGG, i7_2 = GGTTATAA, i7_3 = CCAAGTCC …; 46 provided)

components.name: inDrop common handle
components.role: 15-bp common sequence that anneals to the bead-derived PCR handle; the i7 read then reads BC1 + W1

oligos.notes: Extended Data Fig. 1b: 'the index i7 read, which usually only contains barcode 1 (inDrop v3) — we inserted an 8-bp second index directly after a 15-bp common sequence.' 46 i7 primers in Supplementary Table 12.

oligos.name: rRNA depletion probes (antisense to 18S/28S/5.8S/16S/12S/5S rRNA)
oligos.role: DNA probes hybridized to aRNA rRNA, then cleaved by thermostable RNaseH and removed by DNase — reverse complement of published probes (Adiconis 2013)
oligos.notes: ~200 probes total (Table S12). Representative 5'->3' probe shown (Ribo_Dep_aRNA_18_1). Tiled across 18S (Ribo_Dep_aRNA_18_1..38), 28S (28_1..101), 5.8S (5.8_1..3), mt 16S (16_1..31), mt 12S (12_1..19) and 5S (5_1..3). Used at 25 µM with hybridization buffer (500 mM Tris-HCl pH7.5, 1 M NaCl).

oligos.name: CEL-seq2 / SORT-seq barcoded RT primer (VASA-plate only)
oligos.role: Plate-format barcoded oligo-dT RT primer (0.25 µM, 50 nl/well) — the VASA-plate counterpart of the bead oligo
oligos.notes: VASA-plate uses CEL-seq2/SORT-seq primers (Muraro 2016; Hashimshony 2012); indices available at GSE176588. Read1 = 6-nt UFI + 8-nt cell barcode (384 well barcodes). Sequences not printed in these documents.

final_library.source_label: VASA-drop final dual-indexed Illumina library (schematic; P5 → P7 top strand)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: P5=AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC | i5=[SAMPLE_INDEX:8] | Read1/s5 handle=TCGTCGGCAGCGTC | RTP/RA3-derived=GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA | insert=[CDNA] | poly(dT/dA)=[POLYT] | UFI=[UMI:6] | BC2=[CELL_BARCODE:8] | W1 linker=[SPACER:22] | BC1=[CELL_BARCODE:8] | inDrop handle(rc)=CTGCACCCGACACCC | i7(rc)=[SAMPLE_INDEX:8] | P7(rc)=ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTC                — Read1 / s5 handle; Read1 sequencing primer binds here
GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA         — RTP-derived region (complement of RA3 adapter TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGG)
[CDNA]                        — fragmented total-RNA cDNA insert (Read1, 247 cycles, across gene body)
[POLYT]                       — oligo-dT : poly(A) capture junction (bead poly(dT) x RNA poly(A) tail)
[UMI:6]                       — UFI / unique fragment identifier (read in Read2)
[CELL_BARCODE:8]              — inDrop v3 cell barcode BC2 (read in Read2)
[SPACER:22]                   — inDrop v3 W1 linker (read in i7 index read)
[CELL_BARCODE:8]              — inDrop v3 cell barcode BC1 (read first in i7 index read)
CTGCACCCGACACCC               — reverse complement of the 15-bp inDrop common handle (GGGTGTCGGGTGCAG)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]              — i7 sample index (Index1 read, reverse complement here)
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG      — P7 (reverse complement of VD_ILMN8_i7 primer 5' P7)

evidence.locator: VASA-drop final dual-indexed Illumina library (schematic; P5 → P7 top strand)

library_generation.title: Cell + bead co-encapsulation & lysis/fragmentation
library_generation.summary: Single cells, inDrop-v3 barcoded beads and lysis/fragmentation buffer are co-encapsulated in ~0.55 nl droplets, lysed, and RNA is heat-fragmented.
library_generation.note: Flow-focusing device (80 µm junction, 115 Hz). Lysis mix: dNTPs, IGEPAL-CA630, 40 mM Tris-HCl pH8, First-Strand Buffer, 3 mM MgCl2, Thermolabile Proteinase K. 20 min RT lysis, then 85 °C / 6.5 min fragmentation (no sequence-specific random priming).
library_generation.product:
droplet: [cell lysate + fragmented RNA]  +  [barcoded bead]
  5'- ...NNNNN(RNA fragment)NNNNN -3'   (multiple fragments, no poly(A) yet)

library_generation.title: Barcode photocleavage
library_generation.summary: UV illumination releases the barcoded oligo-dT primer from the polyacrylamide bead into the droplet.

library_generation.title: Picoinjection 1 — end repair & poly(A) tailing
library_generation.summary: T4 PNK repairs fragment ends and E. coli poly(A) polymerase adds a poly(A) tail to every RNA fragment.
library_generation.note: Coalescence-activated picoinjection (250 V, 10 kHz). Enables capture of non-polyadenylated species. 25 min RT + 8 min 37 °C.

library_generation.title: Picoinjection 2 — reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: The barcoded oligo-dT primer anneals to the poly(A) tail and SuperScript III makes first-strand cDNA, tagging each fragment with cell barcode + UFI.
library_generation.note: 50 °C / 2 h then 70 °C inactivation; droplets collected in ~1,000-cell fractions and de-emulsified.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: E. coli DNA Pol I / RNaseH convert the cDNA:RNA hybrid into double-stranded cDNA bearing an intact T7 promoter.
library_generation.product: ds cDNA with [T7 promoter][barcode][UFI] at one end

library_generation.title: In vitro transcription (linear amplification)
library_generation.summary: T7 RNA polymerase transcribes the ds cDNA into many antisense aRNA copies.
library_generation.note: HiScribe/MEGAscript T7. Linear, low-bias amplification carrying barcode + UFI.

library_generation.title: rRNA depletion
library_generation.summary: Antisense DNA probes hybridize residual rRNA-derived aRNA, which is cleaved by thermostable RNaseH and removed by DNase.
library_generation.note: ~200 tiled probes (Table S12); hybridize 95→45 °C, RNaseH 45 °C/30 min, RQ1 DNase 37 °C.

library_generation.title: RA3 3' adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: The pre-adenylated, 3'-blocked RA3 adapter is ligated to the 3' end of each aRNA by T4 RNA Ligase 2 (truncated).

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription with RTP
library_generation.summary: The RTP primer anneals to the ligated RA3 adapter and SuperScript III makes cDNA of the aRNA.
library_generation.note: 65 °C anneal → 50 °C / 1 h; RNaseA cleanup. RTP = GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA.
library_generation.product:
aRNA  3'-...GGAACCGTGGGCTCTTAAGGT -5'
  cDNA       GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-...(gene body)[UFI][BC2][W1][BC1][handle] -3'
              <-------- RTP extension

library_generation.title: Dual-indexed PCR
library_generation.summary: Kapa HiFi PCR with VD_ILMN8 i5 (P5) and i7 (P7) primers adds flow-cell adapters and unique dual sample indices, yielding the final library.
library_generation.note: 2 + 5–6 cycles (55→65 °C anneal). i5 3' handle (TCGTCGGCAGCGTC) primes the RA3/RTP side; i7 common (GGGTGTCGGGTGCAG) primes the bead-handle/barcode side. 0.8x AMPure ×2; Bioanalyzer QC.

library_sequencing.note: Biological read: primer binds the s5 handle, reads through the 22-nt RTP/RA3-derived region and then across the fragmented total-RNA cDNA insert (even gene-body coverage). Adapter/homopolymer trimmed before mapping.

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index sequencing primer = 15-bp inDrop common handle (5'-GGGTGTCGGGTGCAG-3')
library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the top-strand reverse complement of the 15-bp common handle (CTGCACCCGACACCC) and reads leftward, reporting cell barcode 1 (BC1, 8 nt) followed by the W1 linker (22 nt) — the inDrop-v3 first barcode.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...[--W1: 22 bp--][BC1: 8bp]CTGCACCCGACACCC[i7:8] ATCTCGTATGCC... -3'
     I1 (31 bp): BC1(8)+W1(22)  GACGTGGGCTGTGGG-5'  (i7 index primer)
                <------------------
3'- ...[--W1'-------][BC1':8 ]  GACGTGGGCTGTGGG[i7':8]TAGAGCATACGG... -5'

library_sequencing.primer: i5 index sequencing primer (binds s5 handle; NovaSeq forward-strand i5 read)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt i5 sample index; on the NovaSeq 6000 forward-strand workflow the i5 primer binds the s5/Read1 handle and reads the index toward P5.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5:8] TCGTCGGCAGCGTC... -3'
                      i5 primer  <------GACGCTGCCGACGA
                              I2 (8 bp): i5 index
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[i5':8]AGCAGCCGTCGCAG... -5'

library_sequencing.primer: Read2 sequencing primer (binds inDrop-v3 W1 linker)
library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the W1 linker and reads BC2 (8 nt) + UFI/UMI (6 nt). After demultiplexing, the four raw reads are collapsed to two: a combined 16-nt cell barcode (BC1+BC2) + 6-nt UFI, and the cDNA read.

title: VASA-drop
description: VASA-seq (droplet format, "VASA-drop") is a high-throughput single-cell TOTAL RNA-seq assay: after cell lysis, all RNA is fragmented, end-repaired and poly(A)-tailed so that non-polyadenylated as well as polyadenylated transcripts are captured. Fragments are reverse-transcribed with inDrop-v3 barcoded oligo-dT beads (cell barcode + 6-nt unique fragment identifier/UFI), second-strand synthesized, linearly amplified by T7 in vitro transcription, rRNA-depleted, then converted to an Illumina library (RA3 3' ligation → RTP reverse transcription → dual-indexed PCR). Read1 reads the fragmented cDNA insert across the gene body; the cell barcode, UFI and dual sample indices are read in the index/Read2 reads.

reference.label: VASA-seq Supplementary Table 14: reagents and equipment

original_publication.title: High-throughput total RNA sequencing in single cells using VASA-seq

throughput.summary: >30,000 single cells profiled for the mouse atlas (33,662 cells across E6.5–E9.5); high-throughput droplet format at ~$0.11 USD per cell for sequencing-ready libraries.
throughput.cells: 33,662 single cells (mouse gastrulation/early organogenesis); VASA-drop is the high-throughput droplet arm
throughput.rna: Total RNA (poly(A)+ and non-poly(A)); ~9,825 ± 280 genes/cell at 75,000 reads/cell; 56.5% unspliced reads (VASA-drop)
throughput.dna: Dual-indexed Illumina libraries; sequenced on NovaSeq 6000 S2 300-cycle (R1 247 / I1 31 / I2 8 / R2 14)

publication.statistical_model: RNA velocity modeled with scVelo (stochastic and dynamical models); differential splicing-node inclusion (DISNs) modeled by fitting a beta distribution and thresholding CDF-beta values; histone-content bimodality fit with a Weibull distribution; differential expression via two-sided t-test.

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: Fragmentation + end-repair + poly(A) tailing of ALL RNA before RT, giving even gene-body coverage and non-polyadenylated (e.g. histone, sncRNA) capture

other.label: Workflow lineage
other.value: Droplet generation similar to inDrop (Klein 2015); final library resembles CEL-seq/CEL-seq2 (Hashimshony 2012; Muraro 2016)

data_processing.summary: A custom Python/Snakemake pipeline demultiplexes and rearranges the four sequencing reads into a cDNA read plus a barcode/UFI read, whitelists cell barcodes, trims and rRNA-depletes in silico, aligns with STAR, then assigns reads to spliced/unspliced gene biotypes and collapses by strand-specific UFI to build count matrices; downstream analysis covers clustering, RNA velocity and full-length alternative-splicing quantification.
data_processing.statistical_model: no NB/Poisson count model; differential expression by two-sided t-test, differential splicing-node inclusion by a fitted beta distribution (CDF-beta), histone-content bimodality by a Weibull distribution, RNA velocity by scVelo stochastic/dynamical models
